content: AITCs (allyl isothiocyanes) in mustard trigger the nervous system to turn on, affecting appetite.
In a study of 90 humans, mustard reduced the perception of sweetness and increased the perception of salt, potentially decreasing appetite.
Mustard activates TRPV1 receptors, which turn on the sympathetic nervous system, and TRPA1 receptors which receive chemical irritant signals.
Mustard can desensitize us to sweetness, reducing dopamine hit and potentially curbinging appetite.
Special forces operators carry spicy mustard packets mustard packets to stop muscle cramps.
To get the AITCs in mustard, look for stone ground mustard or whole grain mustard or Dijon mustard.
